story: Solstice Roast was imagined for people who want their home coffee routine to feel as intentional as the rest of their space. The brand sits between specialty coffee culture and design-led home goods, so the storefront needs to convert like commerce while still carrying editorial calm.

The product mix is deliberately broad enough to support repeat behavior: dependable coffee bags, convenience-driven formats, and a small tool collection that raises average order value without making the catalog feel noisy.

values.title: Traceable sourcing
values.body: Coffee is chosen for flavor clarity, dependable quality, and origin stories that feel honest instead of exaggerated.

values.title: Fresh roast rhythm
values.body: Twice-weekly roasting gives the store a believable operational cadence customers can understand.

values.title: Object quality
values.body: Tools are selected as countertop objects, not just accessories buried in a navigation menu.

values.title: Clear policy design
values.body: Shipping, returns, privacy, and support are written plainly so the experience feels market ready.

journey.title: Source
journey.body: Blend the catalog around one dependable best seller, one premium single-origin, and one convenience-led ritual format.

journey.title: Roast
journey.body: Present freshness windows, tasting notes, and roast style clearly so every product feels shoppable.

journey.title: Ship
journey.body: Use trust copy, order tracking, and a clean cart to make the store feel ready for real customers.

faqs.question: How fresh is the coffee when it ships?
faqs.answer: Core coffee bags roast twice each week. Most coffee orders leave within 24 hours of a roast window, so freshness language on the storefront feels specific and believable.

faqs.question: Can I subscribe and still change delivery timing?
faqs.answer: Yes. Subscription products are positioned with a flexible promise: pause, skip, or cancel any time. That lowers hesitation without overcomplicating the product page.

faqs.question: Do you offer grind options?
faqs.answer: For this storefront concept, whole bean is the default positioning because it reads more premium. Grind support can be added in Shopify if the final merchant wants it.

faqs.question: Are the tools gift ready?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dawn Pour-Over Kit and Studio Espresso Canister are merchandised as giftable products with premium packaging cues and a 30-day return window.

faqs.question: How does the cold brew concentrate work?
faqs.answer: Each bottle is designed as a quick ritual product: one part concentrate to one part water or milk over ice. It broadens the catalog beyond classic brew enthusiasts.

faqs.question: What if a coffee bag arrives damaged?
faqs.answer: Any damaged or incorrect item is replaced quickly. The shipping page makes that clear so customers are not forced to search through legal copy.

faqs.question: Do you ship internationally?
faqs.answer: This prototype is framed around domestic shipping first. International logic can be added later in Shopify Markets if the store launches globally.

faqs.question: Why are there only a handful of products?
faqs.answer: The brief asks for a clear niche with 3 to 10 products. A tighter catalog often converts better because customers can understand the brand and make decisions faster.

shippingCards.title: Processing
shippingCards.body: Coffee ships within 24 to 48 hours of roast windows. Tools usually leave the studio within 2 business days.

shippingCards.title: Free shipping
shippingCards.body: Orders over $75 ship free within the domestic zone. Smaller orders use a flat standard rate.

shippingCards.title: Returns
shippingCards.body: Unused tools can be returned within 30 days. Coffee is replaced if damaged or incorrect.

notes: Orders placed after Friday roast close may ship the next Tuesday.
Subscriptions renew automatically until paused, skipped, or canceled.
Tracking is emailed as soon as a label is created.
returns: Damaged or incorrect orders: replacement support within 7 days
Coffee products: non-returnable once opened unless quality is compromised

privacy.title: Information we collect
privacy.body: The store collects only the information needed to process demo orders, respond to customer support requests, and improve merchandising. No hidden data practices are presented.

privacy.title: How data is used
privacy.body: Customer details are used for order confirmation, fulfillment communication, support, and optional marketing updates if the visitor subscribes.

privacy.title: Marketing preferences
privacy.body: Newsletter opt-ins remain optional and can be changed at any time. The UI keeps consent separate from checkout behavior.

privacy.title: Retention
privacy.body: Order and account information is stored only as long as needed for operational, legal, and support purposes.

terms.title: Orders
terms.body: Orders are accepted once payment is authorized and an order confirmation is issued. The brand may cancel clearly fraudulent or duplicate orders.

terms.title: Product information
terms.body: Tasting notes, photos, and finish descriptions are presented as accurately as possible, but natural product variation may occur.

terms.title: Subscriptions
terms.body: Recurring coffee subscriptions renew automatically according to the selected schedule until updated by the customer.

terms.title: Liability
terms.body: The store is not responsible for misuse of brewing tools or delays caused by carrier disruption once an order is in transit.

trackOrder.helper: Use your order number to view current status, latest updates, and expected delivery timing.

updates.label: Roast scheduled
updates.detail: Coffee assigned to the next Tuesday production window.

updates.label: Packed
updates.detail: Tools inspected, packed, and labeled for dispatch.

updates.label: In transit
updates.detail: Carrier accepted the parcel and delivery is on time.

checkout.support: This is a demo checkout. No real payment is processed, but the structure is designed to feel complete and trustworthy.
